name: tomat-extension-builtin
description: The everyday starter tools that come with tomat: search the web and read pages, do math, check the date and time, download files, open links, keep memories, and schedule prompts.

tools.name: download_url
tools.description: Download a file from a URL into the user's Downloads folder. Use when the user asks to save or grab a file from a link.

url.description: Absolute http(s) URL of the file to download.

filename.description: Optional filename for the saved file. Defaults to the last segment of the URL.

triggers: download https://example.com/file.zip
save that file to my downloads
grab the pdf from this link
fetch this archive for me
download this file
save this link as a file
download that installer
get me the file at this url
put this download in my downloads folder

read.reason: Check the Downloads folder for existing files and pick a non-clobbering name

env.reason: Honor the user's preferred Downloads folder (Linux/XDG)

tools.name: open_website
tools.description: Open a web URL in the user's default browser. Use when the user asks to visit, open, or go to a website.

url.description: Absolute http:// or https:// URL to open.

triggers: open https://news.ycombinator.com
take me to the github page
visit example.com
go to the docs website
open this link in my browser
pull up the wikipedia page for cats
launch this website
open that url
browse to this site

run.reason: Hand the URL off to macOS's `open` command
Hand the URL off to Linux's `xdg-open`
Hand the URL off to Windows's default protocol handler

tools.name: open_app
tools.description: Open one or more apps on the user's computer by name. Use when the user asks to launch, open, or start apps like a calculator or a browser.

apps.description: App names to launch, e.g. ["Calculator", "Brave Browser"]. Use the app's display name on macOS, its name or executable on Windows, or its desktop id on Linux.

triggers: open the calculator
launch brave and spotify
start the terminal
open my notes app
open spotify
launch the app
start up my code editor
fire up slack
can you open my browser

run.reason: Launch the app on macOS with `open -a`
Launch the app on Windows with `start`
Launch the app on Linux by its desktop id
Fall back to Linux's default launcher

tools.name: open_file
tools.description: Open a file on the user's computer, in a chosen app or the default one. Use when the user asks to open a document, image, or other file, for example a saved report in a specific editor.

path.description: Absolute path to the file to open.

appName.description: App to open the file with, e.g. "Preview". Omit to use the system default app. On Linux a named app is not supported and the default app is used.

triggers: open this pdf
open the report in preview
open that spreadsheet in excel
open my notes file in the editor
open the file at this path
open this document
open that image
open the file for me

run.reason: Open the file on macOS with `open`
Open the file on Windows with `start`
Open the file on Linux with the default app

read.reason: Open any file you point tomat at, wherever it lives on your computer
Open files in your home folder (this is the grant that reaches files on Windows)

tools.name: get_window_layout
tools.description: Read the current position and size of open app windows. Use when the user wants to remember or note their current window layout so it can be restored later. tomat may not be able to read every window; this depends on your operating system.
triggers: remember my current window layout
save where my windows are
note the current screen arrangement
capture how my windows are positioned
record my current window setup
save my current workspace layout
remember how my screen is arranged right now

tools.name: set_window_layout
tools.description: Move and resize open app windows to given positions. Use when the user wants to restore a remembered window layout. tomat may not be able to move every window; this depends on your operating system.

app.description: App or window owner name to move.

windows.description: Target position and size for each window.

triggers: restore my window layout
put my windows back where they were
arrange the windows like before
move my windows back to the saved layout
set up my windows like last time
reposition the windows to my saved arrangement

run.reason: Move and resize windows on macOS
Move and resize windows on Windows
Move and resize windows on Linux (X11)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: List the user's memories by title, kind, and short summary. Use to discover what the user has saved when the relevant-memory summaries do not already cover it, then read_memory the one you need.
triggers: what memories do I have
list my saved notes
which skills are available
do I have a memory about this
show me all my memories
what have I asked you to remember
what do you know about me
what notes have I saved
list everything you remember

memories.reason: List the titles and summaries of your memories

tools.name: write_memory
tools.description: Create a memory, or replace an existing one's content. Memories are notes the user keeps in tomat; for a small change to an existing memory, prefer edit_memory.

title.description: Memory title. Creates the memory if no memory with this title exists.

content.description: Full markdown content for the memory. Replaces any existing content.

triggers: remember that I like my hair yellow
remember that my sister's birthday is in june
remember my wifi password is hunter2
note that I prefer tea over coffee
keep a note that I parked on level 3
write a memory about this
save these notes as a memory
create a new memory called shopping list
replace the contents of my notes memory
store this for later
make a note of this so you don't forget

memories.reason: Create the memory or replace its content

tools.name: edit_memory
tools.description: Change part of a memory by replacing one exact passage. Use for a small edit to an existing memory. The text to find must appear exactly once, so include enough surrounding context to make it unique.

title.description: Title of the memory to edit.

find.description: Exact text to find. Must match exactly one place in the memory.

replace.description: Replacement text. May be empty to delete the found text.

triggers: update the meeting time in my notes memory
fix the typo in that memory
change the deadline in my plan memory
add milk to my shopping list memory
update what you remember about my schedule
correct the address in my contacts memory
remove that line from my notes memory
change my remembered coffee order
actually, remember it's green not yellow

memories.reason: Apply the find/replace edit to the memory

tools.name: read_memory
tools.description: Read the full content of one of the user's memories by title. Use when you need what a memory says to answer.

title.description: Title of the memory to read.

triggers: do you remember the thing I said to you last time
do you remember my wifi password
what did I tell you to remember about the project
recall what I told you earlier
read my meeting notes memory
what does my plan memory say
look at the shopping list memory
check the notes I saved
what did I say about my preferences

tools.name: read_skill_file
tools.description: Read a bundled reference file that ships alongside a skill. Use when a skill's instructions point you to one of its files.

title.description: Title of the skill the file belongs to.

name.description: Name of the bundled file to read (as listed by the skill).

triggers: read the template file from that skill
open the skill's reference file
show me the bundled checklist
read the reference doc that skill mentioned
load the file that skill points to

tools.name: schedule_prompt
tools.description: Propose a prompt for tomat to run on a schedule. At each scheduled time, tomat starts an automated session and sends the instruction. The user reviews and can edit the proposal before it is saved, so fill in your best suggestion. Times are the user's local time.

title.description: Short name for the scheduled prompt, also used as the session title when it fires.

instruction.description: The prompt to send when the schedule fires. Write it as a request from the user, e.g. "Summarize the headlines from my feeds memory."

kind.description: When the prompt repeats: once at a date-time, every N minutes, on weekdays at a time, monthly on a day, or yearly on a date.

at.description: For kind "once": local date-time formatted "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM".

everyMinutes.description: For kind "interval": minutes between runs, 1 or more.

weekdays.description: For kind "weekly": weekdays to run on, 0 (Sunday) to 6 (Saturday).

month.description: For kind "yearly": month to run in, 1 to 12.

day.description: For kinds "monthly" and "yearly": day of the month, 1 to 31 (clamped to shorter months).

hour.description: For kinds "weekly", "monthly", and "yearly": hour to run at, 0 to 23.

minute.description: For kinds "weekly", "monthly", and "yearly": minute to run at, 0 to 59.

runMissed.description: When true, a run missed while tomat was off is made up once on the next start.

triggers: remind me every monday at 9am to fill in the timesheet
every morning give me a summary of my notes
schedule a prompt for tomorrow at 8
check the news once every hour
remind me to take a break every 30 minutes
set up a daily standup summary
run this every week
schedule a reminder for friday afternoon

tools.name: show_memory
tools.description: Show a memory to the user in the chat. Use when the user wants to see a memory itself rather than have it summarized.

title.description: Title of the memory to show.

triggers: show me my meeting notes memory
display the shopping list
open my plan memory in the chat
let me see that memory
pull up my notes memory
show me what's in my ideas memory

memories.reason: Read the memory to display it

tools.name: get_datetime
tools.description: Get the current date and time in the user's local timezone, including the weekday and UTC offset. Use to anchor relative dates like "tomorrow" or "next friday" instead of guessing.
triggers: what time is it
what's today's date
what day is it tomorrow
current date and time
what's the date today
what day of the week is it
what time is it right now
how many days until friday

tools.name: calculator
tools.description: Evaluate a math expression and return the exact result. Supports arithmetic, comparisons, and functions like sqrt, sin, cos, log, min, max. Use for any calculation instead of doing the math yourself.

expression.description: The expression to evaluate, e.g. "(2 + 3) * sqrt(16)".

triggers: calculate 15% of 2400
what is 2 to the power of 20
how much is 144 divided by 12
evaluate this expression
what's the square root of 5041
what is 45 times 89
work out the tip on a 60 dollar bill
add these numbers up for me

tools.name: fetch_webpage
tools.description: Fetch a web page and return its readable text content. Use to read an article, a docs page, or any URL the user mentions.

url.description: Absolute http(s) URL of the page to read.

triggers: read this article for me
summarize https://example.com/post
what does this page say
open the link and tell me the gist
read the contents of this url
summarize this webpage
what's on this page
get the text from this article

net.reason: Fetch the page the user asked to read

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search the web (via DuckDuckGo) and return the top results. Use when the user asks about something current or outside your knowledge, then follow up with fetch_webpage to read a result.

maxResults.description: How many results to return, 1 to 10.

triggers: search the web for deno 2 release notes
look up the weather in amsterdam
find recent news about this
search for how tall the eiffel tower is
google the best pizza in town
what's the latest on this topic
look this up online
find information about quantum computing
